title: Clamato Braise
description: A smoky, fluffy, light Norwegian dish featuring clams that is kosher-friendly and suitable for a light lunch or dinner.
instructions: Rinse the clams under cold water. Pat them dry with a clean kitchen towel.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onions and garlic, cooking until they are soft and golden, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the smoked paprika, tomato paste, diced tomatoes, bay leaf, salt, pepper, and water. Bring to a boil.
Add the clams, lowering them gently into the liquid so they do not touch one another. Reduce heat to low, cover with a lid, and let simmer for about 10 minutes or until most of the clams have opened.
Gently remove the lids every now and then to check on the clams and stir in any juices that collect at the bottom of the pot. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Turn off the heat and serve immediately with crusty bread for dipping.
